pages.text:
__Friday Jan May 31st.__
4.10. we 6ft, Simon, Eve, Charles, Tonic. Heavy Traffic that kept moving to Malvern. It's the Queen's Jubilee weekend. John played in the orchestra & chamber orchestra. He & Rocky have just moved to SH from 168.
__Saturday__ We slept outside no 7. Simon & Eve went to the Priory & then Winter Gardens for speeches, leaving Charles & Tonic in the van at the Medical Centre carpark. The Hills" by John Ireland sounded beautiful, & a Zulu song & "Zadok the Priest". Donna Milroy spoke. Jane & Harry were there. Sunshine
	The drive south to Lyme Regis was absolutely dreadful. M5 totally blocked. We crawled off at __Bristol__ & called in to see Robert & Joan & Naomi [?Grimley]. Then smaller roads to Hook Farm at Uplyme * * *. Crowded.
[pink annotation: Tonic snapped his lead running away from fireworks.]
__Sunday June 2nd__
	Sunshine when we woke. We set off with helmets to Lyme Regis. On the way, Eve went up to Uplyme Church, St. Mary's. Very Friendly. [strikethrough: Queen] Queen's Jubilee; King Solomon. Met [?H]U Iris, 83, organising parenting courses.

I suggested a marquee at the campsite.
	Simon & John & Charles & Tonic went on to the town, & estimated I'd get there 30 minutes before I did. They didn't realise I'd stay for coffee. It was a really pretty walk down to the town beside the river. John met me. We sat on the wall above the beach because dogs are not allowed on the beach. John got us egg & mayonnaise bagels. At one point [strikethrough: Thomas] Tonic was tied up on the wall while we sat on the beach & he fell over the wall & was [?dragged] up by a man. Charles swam.
	We set off to the east [strikethrough: at] as the tide was going out & wore helmets & looked for fossils. Simon & John found some. They were heavy to carry back. We walked back to town & bought ice creams & walked back to the van.
[pink annotation: Fossils from what is now tropic of capricorn, covered by later sand from Shara (before 15- Europeated)]
	We had showers (20p), we have booked a table for supper at 8.15. We ate at the Queen Victoria. Good food. Fireworks at the campsite.
[pink annotation: Section of earth's crust has travelled from Africa → north Lyme Regis since time of dinosaurs.]

1 Asteroceras Obtusum

2 [?Dapedium] [?Plesiosaura]

3 Ichthyosaurus

website www.njpublications damon.co.uk

Lyme Bay

__Monday June 3rd__
	Bleak + cold. We set off for Lyme Regis. It rained + everyone put on rain coats. At 11 we reached the town mill. It was fascinating - a working flour mill with an overshot water mill. [?Restored] a year ago. We 'bought' flour for a donation. They can't sell it because it's different grade flour depending on which volunteer miller is working.
	Then we went to a fascinating [few?] fossil shops. Lovely Lapis Lazuli necklaces. Simon bought John + Charles each little fossils. There was an odd man looking at Tonic + I asked Charles to check he didn't [?let] steal him from where I'd tied him up. He turned out to be a very rude dog warden, a miserable, twisted, old sod, who told me I was committing abandonment, + it allowed him to take Tonic + charge me £60 to get him back etc. + etc... He said Charles had been rude, when all Charles had done was ask other people to watch the warden that he didn't go off with the dog.
	After that it was difficult to find somewhere to leave Tonic + eat. I was very cold. Simon gave me his sweater + we had fish + chips out doors. Then we walked up hill + long roads back to the van. We passed a nature reserve recording the 5 coastal miles of landship 1839. When we reached the van we rested
